title: X-ray Diffraction Crystal Structure Recovery (analytical core)
sub_domain: Powder / single-crystal X-ray diffraction lattice and structure-factor recovery
source_data_quality_note: Analytical core for the XRD pair (paired with L1-519 PWDR wrapper). Recovers crystal lattice parameters, structure factors, and atomic positions from X-ray diffraction patterns via Bragg's law and Rietveld refinement. New to genesis — closest existing siblings are L1-031 X-ray radiography (transmission imaging, not diffraction) and L1-294 DFT (electronic structure, not lattice geometry). Per pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V2_GENESIS_INCLUSION.md: crystal-XRD space-group classification is among the canonical Option-3 recast cases.
related_principles: L1-031 (X-ray radiography — sibling X-ray modality, transmission geometry, informational)
L1-519 (XRD space-group classification PWDR — discrete-readout child)

E.description: X-ray Diffraction Crystal Structure Recovery: analytical forward model for powder or single-crystal XRD. Bragg's law n*lambda = 2*d*sin(theta) governs diffraction angles; structure factor F_hkl = sum_j f_j * exp(2*pi*i*(h*x_j + k*y_j + l*z_j)) governs intensities. For powder XRD: I(2*theta) = K * sum_hkl |F_hkl|^2 * m_hkl * Lp(theta) * P_hkl(eta) * profile(2*theta - 2*theta_hkl); for single-crystal: I_hkl = K * |F_hkl|^2 * Lp(theta) * absorption_correction. Recovery is posed as the joint inverse problem that recovers (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma, atomic_positions, occupancies, thermal_factors) from measured I(2*theta) or {I_hkl}. The forward DAG has 7 primitives with one coupling constraint (n_c = 1): the structure-factor-to-intensity multiplicative coupling (via |F|^2). Difficulty tier delta = 5 (advanced) given the high condition number of structure-factor inversion (phase problem unresolved without anomalous scattering or direct methods). Mismatch parameters: instrumental_broadening, preferred_orientation_powder, sample_displacement, beam_divergence, monochromator_misalignment, background_subtraction_error.
E.forward_model: Bragg's law: n*lambda = 2*d*sin(theta); structure factor: F_hkl = sum_j f_j * exp(2*pi*i*(hx_j + ky_j + lz_j)) * exp(-B_j*(sin(theta)/lambda)^2); powder intensity: I(2*theta) = K * sum_hkl |F_hkl|^2 * m_hkl * Lp(theta) * profile_pseudo_voigt(2*theta - 2*theta_hkl) + background; jointly invert I(2*theta) -> (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma, atomic_positions, occupancies, B_factors)
E.world_state_x: Crystal-structure parameters: lattice constants (a, b, c) [Angstrom], lattice angles (alpha, beta, gamma) [degrees], atomic fractional positions (x, y, z), site occupancies, isotropic or anisotropic thermal factors B (or U_ij).
E.observation_y: Powder XRD pattern I(2*theta) over theta range typically 5-120 degrees at 0.01-0.05 degree step; or single-crystal {I_hkl} for thousands to millions of reflections.

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.bragg_diffraction -> L.structure_factor -> L.intensity_calculation -> L.profile_function -> L.detector_response -> int.angular
coupling_constraints: structure_factor_to_intensity: I_hkl is multiplicatively coupled to |F_hkl|^2 — the structure factor magnitude is observable but its phase is not; this is the canonical 'phase problem' of crystallography that Patterson methods, direct methods, and isomorphous-replacement / anomalous-scattering address

W.regime: Existence guaranteed within declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness conditional: for known atomic composition + Bravais lattice + sufficient resolution, structure refinement is unique up to centrosymmetric equivalence; phase problem requires Patterson methods (heavy-atom), direct methods (Karle-Hauptman maximum-entropy), or anomalous-scattering for ab-initio. Stability conditional with kappa raw ~ 600 (limited by phase problem and reflection-overlap in powder XRD); kappa_eff ~ 80 with Rietveld refinement under structural constraints. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the XRD forward established by Rietveld 1969 (foundational), Hill-Howard 1986 (Rietveld profile refinement), Karle-Hauptman 1956 (direct methods, Nobel 1985), McCusker 1999 (Rietveld guidelines), Young 1993 (Rietveld method textbook), Coelho 2018 (TOPAS), Toby-Von Dreele 2013 (GSAS-II).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + nonlinear refinement [Rietveld profile refinement; least-squares structure refinement] | direct methods [Karle-Hauptman maximum-entropy phase recovery] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [DeepPattern, CrystalNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||(a, b, c, x_j)_hat - true||_2 <= C1 * sigma_intensity / sqrt(N_reflections * SNR_per_reflection). Phase-problem-induced bias adds + C_phase * (1 - heavy_atom_dominance) for ab-initio; Rietveld refinement bias + C2 * |instrumental_broadening| + C3 * |preferred_orientation| + C4 * |sample_displacement|. Achieves q=1 linear convergence under Rietveld (least-squares); direct methods achieve q=2 quadratic when initial phase set is good.
C.complexity: O(N_reflections * N_parameters^2) per Rietveld iteration; direct methods O(N_reflections * log(N_reflections)) per phase-cycle iteration

joint_well_posedness_references: Rietveld HM (1969) A profile refinement method for nuclear and magnetic structures. J Appl Crystallogr 2(2):65-71.
Hill RJ, Howard CJ (1986) Quantitative phase analysis from neutron powder diffraction data using the Rietveld method. J Appl Crystallogr 19(3):173-178.
Karle J, Hauptman H (1956) A theory of phase determination for the four types of non-centrosymmetric space groups. Acta Crystallogr 9(8):635-651.
McCusker LB, Von Dreele RB, Cox DE, Louer D, Scardi P (1999) Rietveld refinement guidelines. J Appl Crystallogr 32(1):36-50.
Young RA, ed. (1993) The Rietveld Method. International Union of Crystallography Monograph No 5, Oxford University Press.
Coelho AA (2018) TOPAS and TOPAS-Academic: an optimization program integrating computer algebra and crystallographic objects. J Appl Crystallogr 51(1):210-218.
Toby BH, Von Dreele RB (2013) GSAS-II: the genesis of a modern open-source all-purpose crystallography software package. J Appl Crystallogr 46(2):544-549.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-031 X-ray radiography is transmission-attenuation imaging (Beer-Lambert through bulk material); sensing_mechanism = transmission attenuation; solution_space = 2D/3D attenuation map. L1-518 XRD is Bragg-diffraction crystallography (Bragg law + structure factor); sensing_mechanism = bragg_diffraction; solution_space = crystal_structure_parameters (lattice + atomic positions). Different physics primitives (L.bragg_diffraction, L.structure_factor vs L.attenuation_projection), different problem class (nonlinear_inverse with phase problem vs linear_inverse). d_principle ~ 0.55 — solidly Distinct.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: X-ray crystallography is the foundational technique of materials science, structural chemistry, drug discovery, and pharmaceutical formulation. Powder XRD is a routine quality-control tool in pharma manufacturing (polymorph identification, amorphous vs crystalline content), cement, metal alloys, semiconductors. Single-crystal XRD is the canonical method for protein structure determination (PDB), small-molecule drug structure, and crystal engineering. Universal commercial systems: Bruker D8, Rigaku SmartLab, PANalytical X'Pert, Stoe STADI MP. UTSW has multiple X-ray crystallography facilities for structural biology (Drug Discovery Institute, BioCryst). PWDR-class downstream Principle (L1-519) provides space-group classification readout.
